cxu_object.claim: When cumulative fees in a single trading session exceed $50 with zero realized P&L, all new entries must be blocked for a minimum of 30 minutes regardless of signal consensus or regime stability. Fee drag of this magnitude indicates a churn loop is active and continuing to trade compounds losses without capturing edge. The circuit breaker resets after the 30-minute cooldown only if the regime has been stable (no transitions) for the entire cooldown period.

supporting_contexts.text: Three consecutive sessions have produced $0 P&L with $24-$72 in fees due to churn loops. In this session alone, $72.78 in fees were generated with no winning trades. A session-level fee circuit breaker would have blocked the third trade ($24.18 fee) after the first two trades already consumed $48.60 in fees with no P&L.
